sublevels.title: Sueños y fantasías: la estructura hipotética

exercises.prompt: Empareja cada palabra con su traducción

exercises.q: If I ___ a million dollars in the lottery, I ___ a large sum to charity.
exercises.hint: No mezcles el presente con would, ni el pasado con will.
exercises.explanation: La combinación de Past Simple en la condición («won») y «would + infinitivo» en la consecuencia («would donate») es la estructura estándar del Second Conditional para escenarios improbables.

exercises.text: If I ___ the lottery, I would buy an island. (ganar, hipotético)
exercises.explanation: La condición del Second Conditional siempre va en Past Simple, aunque no hablemos realmente del pasado: «won» representa una fantasía distante de la realidad.

exercises.q: Which sentence correctly expresses a hypothetical dream, not a real plan?
options: If I win the lottery, I will buy a boat.
If I won the lottery, I would buy a boat.
If I would win the lottery, I buy a boat.
exercises.hint: El sueño improbable necesita pasado en la condición.
exercises.explanation: Un sueño o fantasía poco probable se expresa con el Second Conditional: Past Simple + would. La primera opción es First Conditional (posible) y la tercera mezcla mal los tiempos.

exercises.text: It's just a ___, but I dream about winning the lottery every day. (fantasía)
exercises.explanation: «Fantasy» es la palabra que describe una idea imaginaria y poco realista, como ganar la lotería.

exercises.q: Choose the sentence that does NOT mix First and Second Conditional.
options: If I have more money, I would travel.
If I had more money, I will travel.
If I had more money, I would travel.
exercises.hint: Ambas cláusulas deben pertenecer al mismo condicional.
exercises.explanation: Las dos cláusulas deben coincidir en el mismo condicional: pasado + would. Mezclar presente con would, o pasado con will, es un error típico de examen.

exercises.text: If I won a ___ dollars, I would stop working. (un millón)
exercises.explanation: «A million dollars» es la cantidad hipotética que completa el escenario de fantasía sobre ganar mucho dinero.

exercises.answer: If I won the lottery, I would travel
exercises.explanation: Second Conditional: «won» (Past Simple) en la condición y «would travel» en el resultado hipotético.
Frase descriptiva simple: sujeto + is + «just a fantasy», sin estructura condicional.
«Dream of» va seguido de gerundio: «dream of winning», no del infinitivo «win».

exercises.text: If I won the lottery, I would travel.
It is just a fantasy.
I dream of winning a million dollars.
exercises.es: Sueño con ganar un millón de dólares.

sublevels.title: Riqueza y caridad: would en el resultado

exercises.prompt: Empareja cada palabra con su traducción

exercises.q: If I ___ you, I ___ that job offer on the island.
exercises.hint: Es la fórmula típica para dar consejos.
exercises.explanation: «If I were you» es la estructura típica para dar consejos, combinada con el condicional de resultado «would accept». «If I am you» es físicamente imposible como condición real.

exercises.text: If I were poor, I ___ ask for help. (would, hipotético)
exercises.explanation: El resultado del Second Conditional siempre usa «would» (o «could»), nunca «will».

exercises.q: Which sentence correctly uses 'would' to express what you would definitely do in an imaginary situation?
options: If I were rich, I donate to charity.
If I am rich, I would donate to charity.
If I were rich, I would donate to charity.
exercises.hint: Busca la frase con pasado en la condición y would en el resultado.
exercises.explanation: «If I were rich, I would donate» combina correctamente el pasado de distanciamiento («were») con «would» para expresar una intención segura dentro de la fantasía.

exercises.text: She ___ donate to that charity if she were rich.
exercises.explanation: En el Second Conditional, el resultado hipotético usa «would», que expresa lo que definitivamente haría en esa situación imaginaria.

options: If they asked me, I'd accept.
If they ask me, I'd accept.
If they asked me, I would accept.
exercises.hint: «Would» se contrae en «'d» tras el sujeto.
exercises.explanation: «Would» se puede contraer a «'d»: «I'd accept». La segunda opción mezcla presente («ask») con la forma contraída y la tercera es correcta pero no está contraída.

exercises.text: If I had a ___, I would sail around the island. (barco)
exercises.explanation: «Boat» es la palabra que completa lógicamente la fantasía de navegar alrededor de la isla.

exercises.prompt: Si fuera rico, donaría a la caridad
exercises.answer: If I were rich, I would donate to charity
exercises.explanation: Subjuntivo formal «were» en la condición, seguido de «would donate» en el resultado hipotético.

exercises.prompt: Si tuviera un barco, viajaría a la isla
exercises.answer: If I had a boat, I would travel to the island
exercises.explanation: «Had» (Past Simple) en la condición y «would travel» en el resultado, estructura estándar del Second Conditional.

exercises.prompt: No somos ni ricos ni pobres
exercises.answer: We are not rich or poor
exercises.explanation: Frase descriptiva simple en presente, sin estructura condicional: «are not rich or poor».

exercises.text: If I were rich, I would donate to charity.
exercises.es: Si fuera rico, donaría a la caridad.

exercises.text: If I had a boat, I would travel to the island.
exercises.es: Si tuviera un barco, viajaría a la isla.

exercises.text: If I were you, I would accept that offer.
exercises.es: Si yo fuera tú, aceptaría esa oferta.

sublevels.title: El subjuntivo were: consejos de reyes y presidentes

exercises.prompt: Empareja cada palabra con su traducción

exercises.q: If he ___ the president, he ___ different decisions to support society.
exercises.hint: Se prefiere «were» sobre «was» en exámenes formales.
exercises.explanation: Utiliza el subjuntivo formal «were» para la hipótesis y lo acompaña con «would + infinitivo» en el resultado: «were / would make».

exercises.text: If the situation ___ different, I would make another choice. (subjuntivo formal)
exercises.explanation: En estilo formal, «were» sustituye a «was» para todas las personas en el Second Conditional: «If the situation were different».

exercises.q: Choose the correct formal sentence about an imaginary king.
options: If he were rich, he would live in a palace.
If he was rich, he would live in a palace.
If he were rich, he lives in a palace.
exercises.hint: El inglés formal exige «were» para todas las personas.
exercises.explanation: En el inglés formal y de examen, «were» sustituye a «was» para todas las personas gramaticales en contextos hipotéticos: «If he were rich...».

exercises.text: If she ___ the queen, she could change the laws.
exercises.explanation: La hipótesis formal sobre ser reina requiere «were», seguido del resultado con «could» para expresar capacidad.

exercises.q: Which sentence gives advice using the fixed formula 'If I were you'?
options: If I am you, I would vote for that candidate.
If I were you, I would vote for that candidate.
If I was you, I vote for that candidate.
exercises.hint: Busca la combinación formal completa: were + would.
exercises.explanation: «If I were you, I would vote...» es la fórmula fija para dar consejos en Second Conditional, con «were» (no «was») y «would» en el resultado.

exercises.text: If I were you, I ___ make that decision so quickly.
exercises.explanation: La negación del resultado en Second Conditional se forma con «wouldn't» + infinitivo, no con «won't» (futuro real) ni «don't» (presente).

exercises.prompt: Si él fuera rey, gobernaría el país
exercises.answer: If he were king, he would govern the country
exercises.explanation: Subjuntivo formal «were» con tercera persona («he»), seguido de «would govern» en el resultado hipotético.

exercises.prompt: Si yo fuera tú, votaría por ella
exercises.answer: If I were you, I would vote for her
exercises.explanation: Fórmula fija de consejo: «If I were you, I would vote for her».
Frase descriptiva simple en presente: «They live in a palace», sin estructura condicional.

exercises.text: If he were king, he would govern the country.
exercises.es: Si él fuera rey, gobernaría el país.

exercises.text: If I were you, I would vote for her.
exercises.es: Si yo fuera tú, votaría por ella.

exercises.text: If the situation were different, I would make another decision.
exercises.es: Si la situación fuera diferente, tomaría otra decisión.

exercises.prompt: Empareja cada palabra con su traducción

exercises.q: Imagine how different the future ___ if government choices ___ made by citizens directly.
exercises.hint: Ambas partes deben respetar la correlación del Second Conditional.
exercises.explanation: Ambas partes respetan la correlación temporal hipotética: «would be» para la consecuencia y «were» (pasado pasivo) para la condición.

exercises.text: If I had a vote, I ___ support that choice.
exercises.explanation: El resultado hipotético del Second Conditional usa «would» para expresar una intención clara dentro del escenario imaginario.

exercises.q: Which sentence expresses ability/possibility rather than certain intention?
options: If I were president, I would support that charity.
If we had more time, we could imagine a better future.
If she were queen, she would change the laws.
exercises.hint: «Could» expresa posibilidad, no una intención segura.
exercises.explanation: «Could» expresa que el resultado imaginario sería posible o que tendríamos la capacidad de hacerlo, a diferencia de «would», que expresa una intención más segura.

exercises.text: If we changed the system, society ___ improve.
exercises.explanation: «Could» expresa la posibilidad hipotética de que la sociedad mejorase, sin garantizar el resultado con total certeza.

exercises.q: Choose the sentence with the correct use of a modal followed by the base form.
options: If they had money, they could to travel.
If they had money, they can travel.
If they had money, they could travel.
exercises.hint: Los modales van seguidos de infinitivo sin «to».
exercises.explanation: «Could» es un verbo modal defectivo y debe ir seguido del infinitivo sin la partícula «to»: «could travel», nunca «could to travel».

exercises.text: If citizens governed the world, the ___ would be different.
exercises.explanation: «Future» es la palabra lógica que completa la idea de un mundo hipotéticamente distinto gobernado por los ciudadanos.

exercises.answer: If we voted, we would support that choice
exercises.explanation: «Voted» (Past Simple) en la condición y «would support» en el resultado hipotético.

exercises.answer: We could imagine a better future
exercises.explanation: «Could» expresa la capacidad hipotética de imaginar un futuro distinto, sin garantizar el resultado.

exercises.prompt: La sociedad y el mundo cambiarían
exercises.answer: Society and the world would change
exercises.explanation: «Would change» expresa la consecuencia hipotética segura de una situación imaginaria.

exercises.text: If we voted, we would support that choice.
We could imagine a better future.
Society and the world would change.
exercises.es: La sociedad y el mundo cambiarían.

exercises.prompt: Empareja cada palabra con su traducción

exercises.q: We ___ travel around the world if we ___ a big boat.
exercises.hint: El modal expresa capacidad hipotética; la condición va en pasado.
exercises.explanation: «Could» expresa la capacidad hipotética en la consecuencia y «owned» (Past Simple) es la forma correcta para la condición del Second Conditional.

exercises.text: It would be ___ to travel to every country in the world. (imposible)
exercises.explanation: «Impossible» describe algo que no se puede hacer en la realidad, encajando con el tono hipotético del Second Conditional.

exercises.q: Which sentence correctly imagines an impossible situation?
options: It is impossible to live without rules, but if we live in a perfect world, we won't need them.
It is impossible to live without rules, but if we will live in a perfect world, we wouldn't need them.
It is impossible to live without rules, but if we lived in a perfect world, we wouldn't need them.
exercises.hint: Busca pasado en la condición y would en el resultado.
exercises.explanation: La condición imaginaria e improbable exige Past Simple («lived») combinado con «wouldn't» en el resultado. Las otras opciones mezclan mal los tiempos verbales.

exercises.text: If I ___ more free time, I would travel more.
exercises.explanation: La condición del Second Conditional siempre va en Past Simple: «had», aunque hablemos de una situación presente o futura hipotética.

exercises.q: Choose the sentence that correctly uses 'imagine' to introduce a hypothetical situation.
options: Imagine a situation where you didn't have to work.
Imagine a situation where you don't have to work.
Imagine a situation where you won't have to work.
exercises.hint: El escenario hipotético requiere pasado tras «imagine».
exercises.explanation: «Imagine» introduce escenarios hipotéticos que se describen con Past Simple, igual que la condición de un Second Conditional: «where you didn't have to work».

exercises.text: Imagine a ___ where everyone had enough money. (situación)
exercises.explanation: «Situation» es la palabra que introduce el escenario hipotético que sigue a «imagine».

exercises.answer: We could travel around the world
exercises.explanation: «Could travel» expresa la capacidad hipotética de viajar, resultado típico del Second Conditional.
Imperativo «Imagine» + objeto: «an impossible situation», con el adjetivo «impossible» antes del sustantivo.

exercises.answer: It would be impossible without rules
exercises.explanation: «Would be» expresa la consecuencia hipotética: «It would be impossible without rules».

exercises.text: We could travel around the world.
Imagine an impossible situation.
It would be impossible without rules.
